nameMeanings.name: Donne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Done
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Alfredo, Angel, Berto, Cristobal, Dinora, Eloisa, Francisco, Generoso, Gustavo, Juan.
English: variant of Donne, itself a variant of Dunn .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Donne was born about 1225, in Utkinton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Richard Done. He had at least 1 son with Ellin Swinerton.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Donne est né environ 1225, à Utkinton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Richard Done. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Ellin Swinerton.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Donne wurde um 1225 in Utkinton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Richard Done geboren. Er hatte mit Ellin Swinerton mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Donne è nato il circa 1225 nel Utkinton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Richard Done. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Ellin Swinerton.

ja.Utkinton, Ches, Eng: イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアウトキントン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Donneは約1225年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアウトキントンでRichard Doneの息子として生まれた。彼はEllin Swinertonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Donne(은)는 대략 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 우트킨턴에서 Richard Done의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Ellin Swinerton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

mn.Utkinton, Ches, Eng: Utkinton, Legeceasterscir, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Donne nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Utkinton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Richard Done. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Ellin Swinerton.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Donne родился около 1225 г. в местности уткинтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Richard Done. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Ellin Swinerton.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Donne nació aproximadamente 1225, en Utkinton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Richard Done. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Ellin Swinerton.
